summary.verdict_reason: All 7 hard gates FAIL on the basis of no evidence produced (no B3 backtest results, no B6 walk-forward / framework CV / bootstrap / FWER artifacts). V10-B3 (SMA-33959) is still in_progress and V10-B6 (SMA-33960) is blocked on B3; per issue spec, B7 review cannot proceed before B3 + B6 deliverables land.

scope_lock_vpvr_reversion_banned.evidence: Class is in strategies/team/combo/, not strategies/team/vpvr_reversion_*/; skeleton derives from vol_breakout (compute_vol_bands + bands.is_breakout) cross-coupled with VAL pierce-reject.

scope_lock_val_fade_not_degenerate_rever.evidence: V10 explicitly rejects entries when bands.is_breakout == 'up' (lines 429-431), which prevents double-counting with the breakout family. It is long-only (no short tail) and demands 1h trend == 'up' before any VAL fade. Cross-axis (vol_breakout x vpvr_VAL) is preserved, not a pure reversion.

kiss_dry_solid.evidence: Single skeleton file (vol_breakout_vpvr.py:618 lines) hosts three thin specializations (V9/V10/V11) sharing compute_vol_bands, poc_proximity_ok, val_reject_fade, higher_tf_trend helpers. This is correct DRY here — splitting into 3 files would create 3x drift risk for the shared vol-band logic.

lookahead_discipline.evidence: data_loader.compute_1h_indicators applies shift(1) to ema_50 AND vpvr_val before merging onto 5m bars (lines 188, 210-211). 5m ATR uses standard Wilder with bars [t-13, t]. Strategy's compute_vol_bands anchors to prev_close (df.iloc[-(lookback+1):-1]) to avoid the trivial always-true breakout bug (comment lines 113-117).

no_hardcoded_secrets.evidence: No API keys, no credentials, no URLs in any file. All paths come from config.json or DEFAULT_SOURCE_ROOT.

no_mutation_anti_patterns.evidence: Indicators are computed via df.copy() before mutation; backtest engine uses immutable trade/state dataclasses. compute_vol_bands and indicator helpers do not mutate input frames.

test_coverage.evidence: tests/unit/test_vol_breakout_vpvr.py covers V10 with 6 tests: default_init, required_indicators, registered, long_when_val_reject_with_uptrend, no_signal_when_downtrend, no_signal_when_higher_tf_already_breakout. ALL 18 tests in the file pass (pytest run 2026-07-14 15:42 local, 0.47s, 18/18 PASSED).

fill_convention_disclosure.evidence: config.json declares fill_convention='bar[t].close + cost_per_side' and explicitly notes the bar[t+1].open proxy as documented in strategy.py docstring (lines 28-35). Conservative SL-first ordering on same-bar SL/TP is also documented (line 33).

long_only_decision_disclosure.evidence: Class docstring (lines 348-358) explicitly justifies why V10 is long-only — short tail has historically destroyed Sharpe in cycle-46 family. This is exactly the kind of decision a code review should accept without re-litigating.

minor.summary: Variable `pending_qty_units` is computed with walrus operator but never used; the actual `qty` used downstream is also undefined in the entry-exit branch (the same block computes `qty` again inside the if).
minor.recommendation: Remove dead walrus + redundant `qty` line 299. Replace with a clean qty computation immediately before use.

minor.summary: Entry fill uses `prev['close'] * (1.0 + cost_per_side)` and exit fill uses `prev['close'] * (1.0 - cost_per_side)`. This is the conservative-bar[t+1].open proxy disclosed in config but it double-counts slippage into both sides if the price moves against the fill — i.e. exit uses `prev_close * (1 - cost)` even when the actual exit fill is `bar[t].open`. This is acceptable as documented but is worth noting in the comment for future readers.
minor.recommendation: Add 1-line doc clarification: 'Cost-per-side on entry/exit fill proxy includes both fee + slippage; bar[t].open-vs-prev_close gap is implicitly absorbed.'

minor.summary: signal_id uses `int(datetime.utcnow().timestamp() * 1000)` — fine for backtests but susceptible to collisions when the strategy is called at sub-ms resolution across multiple workers. For paper-trade this is fine.
minor.recommendation: No action. Document the collision risk in class docstring if V10 ever ships to multi-worker paper-trade.

minor.summary: _import_vpvr_value_area silently falls back across two import paths. The fallback also silently swallows Exception. This is fine for cycle-49 but the cycle-49 broken chain (kama_trend_vwap → LHFrameStd) is mentioned only in a docstring — a future agent debugging a missing function would benefit from logging the actual exception.
minor.recommendation: Log the swallowed exception with `logger.warning(...)` at minimum.

next_step_required: Re-run B7 review AFTER V10-B3 (SMA-33959) drops results/v10/{metrics.json, summary.json, equity_BTCUSDT.csv, trades_BTCUSDT.csv, walk_forward.json} AND V10-B6 (SMA-33960) drops results/v10/{walk_forward_summary.json, bootstrap_ci.json, framework_cv.json}. Per the issue spec, do NOT advance until every gate is measurable.
